sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application No. 59 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No. 27. 2. For orders on CMA No. 494/24 (Exemption) 3. For hearing of main case. 31.10.2025 Mr. Huzoor Bux Leghari, Commissioner, MTO is present. On 23.10.2025, the following order was passed:- “23.10.2025 Mr. Sajjad Ali Solangi, advocate for applicant. Learned counsel remains unable to articulate any question of law. Since it is a matter of public revenue, applicant, Commissioner Inland Revenue, Zone-V, Medium Taxpayers office be in attendance on the next date. To come up on 30.10.2025.” On 30.10.2025, the following order was passed:- “30.10.2025 Mr. Sajjad Ali Solangi, advocate for applicant along with Mr. Huzoor Bux Leghari, Commissioner, MTO. Pursuant to the last order, Mr. Huzoor Bux Leghari, Commissioner, MTO is present; however, unable to articulate any question of law arising from the impugned judgment. At his request to come up tomorrow i.e. 31.10.2025.” Today, learned commissioner is present and has not prepared any reformulated questions of law. Accordingly, this reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in the connected file. Judge Judge Ayaz p.s.
